title: AI Power for Small Business

modules.title: Mapping AI to Your Business Needs
modules.sub: Pinpoint where AI will deliver real value for you
modules.description: Learners audit their current business operations to identify pain points and opportunities where AI can have immediate impact. They use a structured framework to prioritize AI use cases based on effort, cost, and potential return — leaving with a personalized opportunity map.

modules.title: Automating Customer Service Cheaply
modules.sub: Set up affordable AI tools that handle customer questions
modules.description: Learners explore low-cost and free AI chatbot and helpdesk tools suited to small business budgets and evaluate which fits their customer communication channels. They configure a basic automated response workflow, reducing the time they personally spend on routine customer inquiries.

modules.title: AI Marketing on a Small Budget
modules.sub: Generate compelling content and campaigns without an agency
modules.description: Learners use AI writing and image tools to produce social media posts, email campaigns, and ad copy tailored to their brand voice. They compare free and low-cost marketing AI platforms and build a repeatable content production workflow they can run independently.

modules.title: Inventory and Operations Forecasting
modules.sub: Use AI to predict demand and reduce costly guesswork
modules.description: Learners apply accessible AI forecasting tools to their own sales or inventory data to generate demand predictions and spot seasonal patterns. They practice interpreting AI-generated forecasts and translating those insights into concrete ordering and staffing decisions.

modules.title: Affordable AI Tools Compared
modules.sub: Cut through the noise and choose tools that fit your budget
modules.description: Learners work through a side-by-side comparison of popular AI tools across key small business functions, evaluating price, ease of use, and fit. They apply a decision rubric to their own context and select a shortlist of tools to carry forward into their 90-day plan.

modules.title: Protecting Customer Data Responsibly
modules.sub: Handle data ethically and stay on the right side of regulations
modules.description: Learners identify which customer data their AI tools collect, store, or process and assess the associated privacy and compliance risks. They implement a simple data hygiene checklist and review vendor privacy settings to ensure their AI usage aligns with basic legal and ethical standards.

modules.title: Avoiding Common Small Business AI Mistakes
modules.sub: Learn from others' failures before making costly errors yourself
modules.description: Learners examine real-world case studies of small businesses that misapplied AI — through over-automation, poor tool fit, or neglected oversight — and diagnose what went wrong. They conduct a self-assessment of their planned AI adoption to catch and correct similar pitfalls before they arise.

modules.title: Building Your 90-Day AI Plan
modules.sub: Walk away with a concrete, sequenced action plan to execute
modules.description: Learners synthesize everything from the course into a structured 90-day implementation roadmap tailored to their specific business, budget, and goals. They set measurable milestones, assign tool selections to each phase, and define simple success metrics so they can track progress and adjust confidently.

synopsis: Small business owners don't need a tech team to start using AI — they need a practical, plain-English guide to the tools that actually move the needle. This course delivers hands-on guidance tailored to real small business challenges and budgets.
rationale: With the lowest corpus similarity score of 0.53 against only a broad everyday-AI course, and steady demand at 5/10, small business owners represent an underserved and high-motivation audience whose specific AI literacy needs are currently invisible in the curriculum.
